intro: Ammoselinum butleri, also known as Chinese angelica or dong quai, is a perennial plant in the Apiaceae family native to China and other parts of East Asia. It has been traditionally used to promote women's health, relieve menstrual cramps, and alleviate menopausal symptoms, making it a valuable herb for supporting reproductive health. The bioactive constituents of Ammoselinum butleri include ferulic acid, coumarins, and essential oils, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
